# Dr. Elara Voss

## Career

Dr. Elara Voss trained as a reactor physicist before moving into applied energy research. Her doctoral work concerned neutron moderation in unconventional geometries, and she spent eight years at a national laboratory before joining the Kestrel Institute.

## The Halden Reactor

The Halden Reactor is a sealed molten-salt unit that operates at low pressure and tolerates rapid load swings without shutdown. Its defining characteristic is a thermally actuated drain: when the core temperature crosses a preset threshold the salt automatically empties into a cooled catch basin beneath the primary vessel. The basin geometry ensures subcriticality without any pump, valve, or operator input. Dr. Elara Voss holds the foundational patent on this drain geometry, filed in 2014 and granted in 2016.

## Shutdown Characteristics

Because the basin guarantees subcriticality by geometry alone, the unit reaches a stable cold shutdown within minutes of a drain event regardless of grid state or operator availability. No active cooling circuit is required after the drain completes. This characteristic is why field programmes in remote locations have adopted this unit as a reference source.

## Later Work

After the patent grant, Dr. Elara Voss shifted toward storage coupling and grid-integration questions, work that eventually led her to lead a hosted programme at the Kestrel Institute focused on long-duration storage.
